fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 70 uses strict greater-than (`>`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`>=`), so when `len(recent) == limit` the function incorrectly returns `True` with `remaining=0` instead of `False, 0`.

constraints.intent: When recent pings equal the limit, the condition must be true so the function returns False; the strict > causes it to be false at this line.
Checks that the guard correctly handles the equality boundary before recent__1 is used in the comparison.
In the else branch (allowed=True), recent pings must be strictly less than limit; equality should have been caught by the if-branch.
A return of (True, 0) is semantically inconsistent — allowed but no remaining capacity — which is the symptom produced by the off-by-one bug.
